channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: both Swift and DTCC are going live right now — 143 replies — 411.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
both Swift and DTCC are going live right now

Open thread • Replies: 143 • Updated: 2026-07-16 10:06 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62466455
Price?

62466457
>>62466455
Suppressed,

62466461
And literal top-buying turned full time fudders are finally capitulating.

Imagine subjecting yourself to all the pain and never seeing it through the end. I gave myself 6 months after SWIFT and DTCC are live to see if the token shoots up, and then decide wether to sell half my stack of just staking rewards.

But to sell RIGHT BEFORE the literal ANNOUNCED DATES of the two biggest financial behemoths' launches? That's crazy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62477138
>>62477021
Its a flat fee in dollars, converted to link, paid to node operators, sold for dollars. Its an unneeded hop.

62477152
>>62477094
You didn't say "miners don't want to sell at a loss", you said "miners are the ones who put a price tag on BTC".

>>62477138
>Its an unneeded hop
It really isn't. For the simplicity and uniformity of the basic infrastructure alone it makes perfect sense to use a single token.
And even notorious Link fudder Vitalik admits the economic (dis)incentive aspect makes a token needed as well.

62477191
>>62477152
>And even notorious Link fudder Vitalik
He is jelly , he fiddled with the whole proof of work thing while he could have printed out of thin air and just dumped annualy in the form of token unlocks.

The only good thing with the link conversion/sale is the node operators can sell it for thei local jeetcoin currency.

62477445
>>62477152
>You didn't say "miners don't want to sell at a loss", you said "miners are the ones who put a price tag on BTC".

62477487
>>62477445
If you think those are the same thing I can see why you are frustrated with someone pointing out the error to begin with.

The market decides the price. Miners have 2 options:

1. Sell now to cover costs
2. Eat the costs and wait for the market to improve

Neither scenario is them setting the price. Scenario 2 is miners speculating with coins the same way any market participant does, except their tokens were previously uncirculating.

62479611
>>62477445
Those two statements are WORLDS apart.

You are seriously stupid, I want you to know that so maybe in the future you won't post as much out of embarrassment (slim chance, I know).

62481334
>>62477487
Dude, the BTC network CANNOT be sustainable if the price of BTC doesn't factor in the cost of maintain the network. No market in existence is mutually exclusive from the costs of operations to operate the economy the market is built on top of. You have to have some counter force to weaken its force to manipulate it in your favor.

Miners are the ONLY ones injecting new BTC into the network. I'm not saying they are the sole arbiters that dictate the price of BTC (you have to as retarded as >>62479611 to interpret it this way else miners would shoot it up to $1M a BTC already) but they sure as hell can influence the price purely by their position. This is why the inflation comparison between L1s, BTC notably, and Chainlink are flawed. New link enter the market through Node operators selling and also CLL selling. More damningly, the payments made to CLL to access the services provided by the node operators aren't in Link nor are they converted in am manner to transparently tie the link to the network usage by having the revenue and subsequently market pressure buy be a representation of usage. 62479611 can't refute this thus he has to resort to ad hominem attacks and making mountains out of molehills like the cocksucker he is.

62481363
>>62477191
Why was vitalik so seething about that?

62481409
>>62481334
you went from
>miners are the ones who put a price tag on BTC
to
>miners can influence the price of BTC

These two statements are once again worlds apart.
And the only way miners can actually influence the price of BTC is negatively: the less they sell the less they negatively impact the price.

Just eat the fucking L you sperg.

62482916
Posting on the 15th! Nothing happened!

62482918
today is the day, fellow stinktards! link will finally moon and yes, you guessed it: our real lives can BEGIN

62483211
>>62466457
Yeah, what's the excuse now that it is supposedly in use? Lol They aren't suppressing the price. There is zero buying volume you fucking retard. link is downstream global liquifity and macro/geopolitical structures impacting its flows. Literally all crypto functions this way and resistance bands for each crypto individually establish in interconnected ways for this reason. You are literally just looking at one of them and being like "see see!!!! Look, mine is special!" and ignoring all the others. You aren't noticing anything anyone is missing, you are REALLY retardedly hyper-fixating on one and missing the cross-market theme and thinking it is longterm edge despite no mechanism for actual price growth or even sustenance being built just...cuz. You are LITERALLY basing ALL of your thinking on the lowest IQ analysis imaginable of watching one coin's chart in ignorance of all the others and getting hyped by announcements that mean nothing to token holders by way of value or revenue which are literally written for (((Sir Gay))) to sell to (You) with a lift in retail buying orders. It is literally that simple. And its equilibrium is sinking down and to the right, still unable to support $30 of network ops with revenues and zero planned or even hypothesized build of implementation to change that. They are not building for you, they are funding their build and salaries and exit through you. Lol Learn literal econ 101

62483231
>>62483211
Hi verbo, what's the status on that Swift takeover?

>now that it is supposedly in use
That suppression pic is about Link being used in DTCC's collateral appchain. That's not what launched today.

62484683
Sslinkie1 has been going crazy in these biz comments accusing everyone of being an XRP holder recently
Weird way of coping

62485247
>>62466457
Youre mentally ill if you think bitcoin went down because of link. Holy shit this level of derangement is seriously scary. Dudes like you are actually out there roaming the streets along with me. Absolute mindboggling stuff

62485249
>>62485247
>he just found out that Link baggies are schizos
and nothing you will say to him will change his opinion because he's literally mad

62485317
>>62485247
Bulgarians, Italians, and bitcoin whales are doing everything they can to stop Chainlink.

62485997
>>62485247
>>62485249
>>62485317
You're right it was just a coincidence.

And this too >>62467657
And this >>62468805
And pic related
And the dozens (if not hundreds) more of these. Pretty much all major Chainlink news and announcements.
All coincidences.

62486001
>>62485997
and this one too

62486012
>>62486001
oh and this one

62486018
this is happening btw
DTCC live in production, with Chainlink.

62486482
>>62486018
They are pumping bitcoin to suppress chainlink.

62486630
so now DTCC will just gradually pump link's price forever right sinkies? ya'll gonna make it?
you'd think linkies would be more enthused. maybe deep down they know this means nothing

62487460
>>62486630
Cry along now, xerpxerp

62487501
>>62466645
That’s called projection. You’re clearly a xrp retard. Lying retards. Brad clearly stated to CLG that youre all cash cows

62487520
>>62467154
You need to look into how much failed trx cost banks each year. That alone is a huge incentive. Then there’s also a huge suit of frictionless financial products.
Kek fuddies

62487535
>>62487501
>Brad clearly stated to CLG that youre all cash cows
that was the fat slimy kike David, not Brad, but yeah, he literally told people
>well XRP dropping like a stone is GOOD ackshually because... you can buy more at a cheaper price!!
>XRP is not a sercurity. Ripple's XRP is Ripple's to use to advance their business interests. If you'd prefer exposure to all of Ripple's business, you can buy stock in Ripple.

And the crippletard cucks will take it in the ass like the good, raped slaves they are LMAO

62487575
>>62487501
Isn't it crazy to think that all the Link fud these past 6-7 years came from xerpies?

Even Adem is just out in the open with it now; this whole thread is him defending XRP from getting called out over the xerpie DTCC delusion: >>62484143

62487582
>>62487535
>>62487575
sirs XRP is bad token, please invest in Chainlink if you want the village well fed.

Praise krishna

62487621
>>62487582
>xerpie #36 enters the Chainlink-Swift-DTCC chat

You guys have been living in link threads this past half decade.

item.title: so when DTCC and Swift actually start using chainlink for their every day operations, wouldn't that mean the chainlink L… — 87 replies — 348.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
so when DTCC and Swift actually start using chainlink for their every day operations, wouldn't that mean the chainlink L…

Open thread • Replies: 87 • Updated: 2026-07-16 10:24 UTC

OP

so when DTCC and Swift actually start using chainlink for their every day operations, wouldn't that mean the chainlink LLC would be making boatloads of cash from the fees? And wouldn't these fees be used to buy chainlink on the open market, thus impacting the price positively?

Early replies (first 3)

62476682
>>62476675
He is not necessary.

62476690
>>62476675
your logic is absolutely correct. This will result in BTC dumping like its life depends on it.

62476730
>>62476675
Inb4 tranny anime poster

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62481178
>>62479122
BOOOOOOMMMMMMMMMM

62481198
i still believe

62481201
DTCC is about XRP

62481213
>>62479348
TWO MORE WEEKS

62481227
>>62479553
I've been looking for this meme for years. Thank you, Link Chad

62481243
>>62476675
I only have ~10k chain link fuck my life. I’ve been waiting for like 1 year for this coin to go to $5.00 again. Please God

62481293
>>62479346
You mean like how Sergey and company dumps millions of tokens per month to pay their employees. Lol so far there is more downward pressure on price being created by the link team itself that Bitcoin won't even need to dump.

62481313
>>62481293
How's the Swift takeover going, xerpie?

62481330
>>62481211
>>62481293

ruh roh

62481492
>>62481243
your wish will be granted, you're actually lucky because you'll be able to slurp at $4-$5 for the next couple of years

62481602
>>62477267
Because we all already know they are fake
Xrp and link holders really belonh with each other

62481670
>>62480232
>How's the Swift takeover going, cripplet?
>Where are the "over 100 banks" bro?
>Where are the "largest Swift-enabled financial institutions"?
>It's been 8 years, where is everything?

I dont know what you're even talking about.
Im asking the same questions and CLL have not achieved anything.
What is there left for them to do that will increase the token price

62481678
>>62481602
Watching people fight over XRP and LINK is like watching people fight over whether Marvel or DC is better. they're both for faggots and you'd have to be literally retarded to like either

62481749
>>62481670
You're very clearly the verbose cripple shill.
You've been obsessed with CLG ever since he started shitting on Xerp five-six years ago.

62481761
>>62481678
it's not like that at all
marvel and DC are both successful franchises
xrp is just a scam

62482151
>>62481749
You're talking on fucking riddles you retard. I don't even know who xerp is.
I've literally just debunked all of your claims and now you're making shit up

62482524
>>62479364
>XRP
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM SHAKALA. I am new to 4chan but have been loving this meme lately! I read on reddit yesterday that this would happen and I boughted (but unfortunately the price basically immediately soon after). I was still so pumped I went to Costco and got a slice of pizza and sat facing those walking out looking at everyone walking by knowing and rye-ly smiling knowing none of them understood what is happening. I felt like Brownstone Capital with Ben Rickerts in the Big Short. I had to stop myself from celebrating because I remembered they were missing out and I wanted to feel the same type of compassion they were reminded to feel by Ben in that moment so I finished my pizza solemnly leaving a bite out of clenched stomach respect for them not being able to finish my meal, grabbed my bag, put my hood up, and walked out nodding to the door inspector knowing looking at them knowingly and humanly as I walked out with them smiling not knowing beneath my shades how I was really seeing them. It was an amazing experience and it felt like everyone was watching but I know they weren't because the thing that made it special was that they don't understand or see. I will come back and tip the kitchen staff handsomely (especially the girl who I like but have not had the courage to ask out....yet) when we are making bookoo bucks and I can afford to own the whole store and promote her. and then smirk as I tell her I will sell the store, non-chalantly, so that I can ask her so she would feel comfortable if I ask her out on a date after I made her general manager of the store. I can't wait!

62483155
>>62482524
Anyone reading this linkie written post, for your own entertainment also see:
https://warosu.org/biz/thread/62479839#p62482964

62483160
>>62481678
Not everything is a sunken dichotomy and being non-committal doesn't make you transcendent, it just makes you a more narcissistic NPC. link is trash. XRP is The Standard.
>>62481749
>Everyone who disagrees with me is the same person
He is not me, I am me.

62483496
I think it depends on how the fee model actually works. Institutional adoption doesn't automatically mean constant buy pressure unless part of the revenue is consistently tied to acquiring LINK from the open market. I'd wait to see how the economics play out instead of assuming usage alone guarantees price appreciation. I'm also interested in how Candora Agent will help analyze market events and sentiment once it launches.

62483559
>>62482151
>I've literally just debunked all of your claims
kek yeah, you debunked the basic utility of the Link token by saying the OG whitepaper doesn't matter.
You're a fucking loon.

And you've been crying about CLG and his wallets for many years while also shilling XRP with the same ID.
We all know it's you.

62484352
>>62483559
I've not mentioned XRP once
This is why LINKies are doomed to be poor and why Sergey has a net worth of 700 million. The lack of basic comprehension and not being able to see the truth infront of them
Chainlink Labs scammed you and you defend them on anonymous message boards
Theyre not gonna share that revenue with you bro

62484403
did u guys see this lmao >>62484143

62487489
>>62484352
>Theyre not gonna share that revenue with you
Chainlink revenue is shared with Link holders because more demand for Chainlink products directly translates to more demand for the Link token.
Because CL nodes must be paid in Link for literally everything they do, and there isn't a single Chainlink product, service, or feature that does not involve CL nodes.

If you wanna talk about revenue issues, the absolute worst mismatch I can think of in the crypto space is happening at Ripple.
They actively dump XRP and use that money to buy companies that don't use XRP at all but that boost revenue for Ripple shareholders.

62487541
What I like about these threads is how little people understand Link and what it does. It’s like watching retarded kids play football.

62487576
>>62487541
Chainlink makes Sergey rich and happy. Baggies get to defend him on 4chan.

62487620
>>62479415
>firsties
subhuman spotted kek. no white man ever uttered the word 'firsties'/'firstie'
you're the 36yo third worlder that shits himself because irritated colon and a cocaine addiction, aren't you? istg you vermin are so easy to spot

62487642
>>62487620
I don't even care about the whole fuddie-linkie war because its cringe but fudders turned out to be all european (apart from Adem who's turkish)

Linkies are mostly brown favela monkeys or literal indians that missed the bitcoin train. Check any Link conference

62487665
>>62487642
>check any LINK conference
>only posts the 2 or 3 community shitter events that were held outside of the West 5 years ago
>ignores literally all other events
You are persistent, I'll give you that. But what a strange hobby.

62487669
>>62487665
you got pissed because he called you a thirdie because you most likely are one

item.title: Everest MiCA CASP — 84 replies — 336.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Everest MiCA CASP

Open thread • Replies: 84 • Updated: 2026-07-16 10:18 UTC

OP

I want to personally thank every Everest ID hater in here, talking to you Link baggies. They secured MiCA CASP last week.

A single MFSA license is valid across 27 EU states, reaching ~450M people. Of the ~283 CASPs on the ESMA register, nearly all are exchanges or brokers. Everest is one of the rare licensed infrastructure providers: identity and payment rails, not another venue.

Enjoy it furries

Early replies (first 3)

62461998
>>62461988
I thought it would have mooned harder from the news. What gives

62462006
It is because we are meant to suffer as token holders just like the Linkies

62463131
>Unlisted from coingecko
Lmao!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62480654
lol @ the shameless attempts of keeping this thread alive. Every time it’s about to archive, some “new” anon comes in to bump it to the top. No one is buying your bags retard.

62480772
Keep coping buddy, ID will dildo in your face one day and then you are the clown

62481587
>>62461988
If you’re seeing this God is giving you a chance
https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/crypto/articles/u-uk-back-cross-border-162721181.html

62481591
>>62481587
https://everestdotorg.medium.com/everest-newsletter-january-2026-530f5990f65c

62481712
>>62481587
>>62481591
>Every time it’s about to archive, some “new” anon comes in to bump it to the top
Like clockwork kek

62481767
>>62481712
Building Where Demand Is Real
One of the clearest signals we saw in 2025 was growing traction in the foreign exchange and cross-border payments markets. Across multiple conversations with providers, a consistent need emerged: the ability to compliantly onboard users and rapidly convert fiat currencies into USD-backed stablecoins, without friction or regulatory ambiguity.
Everest worked to address this need. During the year, we supported and tested flows involving EUR, GBP, NOK, BRL, and other fiat currencies, converting into USD-denominated stablecoin rails with speed, auditability, and compliance front-and-center. For FX and payments providers, speed matters — but compliance matters more.

62481908
This thread will age like fine wine. People in here talk about 10x from XRP or Link when 1000x 1m mc is staring them right in face.
A private GDPR-compliant IPFS datastore and cross-chain zero-knowledge proofs let Everest keep identity attributes encrypted at rest and in flight. That rare marriage of privacy architecture with a MiCA CASP license is what TradFi institutions need to integrate.
Enjoy it.

62482011
>>62481908
>maybe if I start spamming buzzwords across multiple IDs, people will fall for it again
2021 was 5 years ago. Let it go

62482121
Holy crap you are dumb, everyone else in here are ID enjoyers or cursing it to hell. No in between. That is the power of Everest. Some dude lost so much money he put picture of Bob under rock in his bum fuck island country and called it a hex kek. Maybe the hex is 1 cent ID coin forever

62482133
This is where the chads ape, shitcoin down 99.8%, MiCA CASP just issued, so they do not have to worry about AI taking their job and being on Elon’s high universal income AKA you do everything we say or we blacklist you. Peter Thiel as well you will have to obey him too if no ID bag. It begins.

62482516
>fat tranny still rambling about holding a scam coin

lol

lmao

62482527
This is regarding Bitboy marketing Everest 3 years ago right? If so, rest assured, no more fat trannies on the team as of past few years innit

62482743
member when they onboarded some emerald mine and gave them millions in free tokens which they dumped on holders i member

62483435
>>62482743
this cant be real lmao

62483487
It’s a lie lol, mining company bought 1 million ID at 25 cents via otc to use Everest for their RWA. That 1 million ID was never sold on market….retards

62484212
BUMP! That was a close one! Cmon eversisters, we have to do better at keeping this thread alive

62484271
The price keeps rising holy shit

62484289
Don’t fall for this transphobic scam. Ever since my MtF surgery Bob refuses to update my biometrics and now I have no access to tokens.

62484582
>>62484271
He’ll yea, brother! We went from $1.22m to $1.28m! Nocoiners on suicide watch! Watch them cope, seethe, and dial the number 8! Now check my freaking digits!

62485043
>>62484582
Check now :) just getting started too

62485430
>>62485043
>$1.2m to $1.4m
>smugface.jpeg
Heck freaking yea. We are sooo totally gonna make it! I can hear the haters dial8’ing as we speak ;^)

62485733
Haters better get their rope ready

62485738
May the 4 Chan mods please pin this super important thread?

62485915
>>62485733
That’s right, BJ Bob. I just bought 150 shares of CopeRope™ because the HATERS are so totally going to rope themselves haha

62486225
I’ve tried to rope the last 5 years to save myself the pain from holding this dog shit token but here we are today kek

62486253
>>62486225
Hey eversister, you forgot to change your ID. Please delete before others see this

62486286
Holy retard yes I’m the original poster. I’m not a feg like some of you 4channers with 10 alts to boost your little egos, grow up tardos. Bob will shit in your faces and my years of mental disease will magically disappear

62487330
Just commenting during my 3 am middle of night parasite dump, ID still exists

62487641
>>62487330
Thanks for saving the thread. I was asleep and didn’t wake up in time to bump it lol

62487651
fake buys going on rn

item.title: AirFox — 35 replies — 140.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
AirFox

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-07-16 10:22 UTC

OP

Any qrd on what’s going on with this thing? What exactly are these guys hoping to accomplish? Crypto is cooked and they keep trying to change this project in ways that don’t seem to matter. The TG link also doesn’t work anymore.

Early replies (first 3)

62481067
>>62480640
>2 hours later
>0 replies
jfc it’s worse than I thought. These threads used to reach bump limits after a couple hours.

62481088
>>62480640
it's a dead shitcoin, the devs are just waiting for the few final bagholders to give up so they can wrap it up and enjoy the money they made selling this shit

62481280
>>62480640
its just a rebrand and a relaunch with shifty eyes about robinhood instead of base.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62481437
>>62481390
I’m convinced that these guys don’t know wtf they are doing or that they even have any goals. They keep shifting the goalpost and jumping from one idea to another. I feel sick to my stomach for even holding all these years.

62481505
>>62481390
>Due to ongoing market retractions, we have to reduce the depth of our main liquidity pool to sustain development. This means increased volatility at lower token prices
lol

62481552
I'm hoping things will turn around but a rebrand and a move to the hottest new chain is usually a sign of a dying shit coin.
>t. top 30 holder

62481688
>>62481415
>I thought they were supposed to be chain agnostic or whatever? What are they trying to do with robinhood? And what ever happened with stratus? Isn’t that supposed to be some kind of dex in itself?
I’m out of the loop as well. Stratus was supposed to be backed by some big VC and it was supposed to be the solution to all things on chain. The bridge was also supposed to be the solution to all things bridge related. Now I’m reading that the team wants to be part of robinhood some how?

62481700
>>62481390
>why they migrated the liquidity from the old token and then only seeded the new one with a fraction of it.
typically we call that a scam

62481704
>>62481552
I got in just after launch with 60m and halved that since then. All signs are pointing to this project dying off. There is no other way around it desu. 2023 and early 2024 were comfy. The team could have done stuff differently back then to get this shilled better outside of biz but for the most part, crypto is fucking dead. That’s out of their control. It would be better if they can come up with a way to just sunset the project and finish it off. Sell everything off and distribute the eth to everyone who held on all these years rather than wallets slowly draining away whatever is left of the liquidity.

62482029
WTFWT HAHAHAHAHA STIXIL YA MADLAD I KNEW YOU COULD DO IT!!!! I NEVER DOUBTED YOU WAGMI! FUCK ALL YOU HATERS!

62482206
>hey guise whats dis aviator coin im totally not stixil looking for more poor suckers as exit liq from my 3 year old scam

62482270
>>62482206
I doubt that's the case. Magic definitely used to shill here and I'm almost certain the first threads were from him with all the "insider" shit he would post. There's simply no more liquidity to farm from this board. It's dead along with crypto.

62482398
>>62482029
Unless we break past $1m again, I’m not falling for this fake pump. It’s probably the same front running bots that come and go

62482721
>+52%
>$5k volume
Yea I’m definitely falling for that one. BTC is slowing down again which means a market wide dump is about to happen. Avi is going back to $400k. Check em

62482861
this coin is driving me insane

62482868
>>62482861
Why don't you play some FUD Free Fox in the aviator arcade if you need to blow off some steam?

62482872
>>62481704
>2023 and early 2024 were comfy

not really (sure the chart went up), they hedged way too much with Base and they ghosted the fuck out of them. the shitposting was fun

>>62482868
ill play that penguin game too

>>62481415
>$300k mcap
that's nothing, if it hits 5-10m without any massive selloff to 1m ill be interested

62483375
>>62482872
I acknowledge that the team messed up along the way but it was still a comfy hold early on. The tg was chill, the team was active, the AMAs were something to look forward to, and overall everyone had hope. Then all the “early frens and supporters” and paw drama started happening, the kerasu saga, the obvious signs they gave up on the arcade in favor of the bridge that no one cared for, the on chain summer BS, ambiguous messages trying to lead us on, etc. It just continued going down hill from there.

62483861
kek the fake pump did t even hold for 24 hours. I remember them lasting for at least a few days, even a couple months before they dumped. This isn’t looking so good

62484514
kek jimmy won.
matthew red won.
cryptoempress won.
rowdy randy won.
maxpain won.
sumdog won.
shadowlord won.
aviator lost. airfox? LMAO you have no liquidity. VC abandoned stratus. Remember the 1 cent per AVI? LOL. magic is gone btw? is mitch gone? is stixil the only one that remains? holders quit their jobs at tim hortons for this coin and THIS is how you repay them?

62484570
don't listen to the haters stixil. If you keep trying you will eventually succeed and show everyone was wrong. I do suggest putting back the liquidity, its a huge red flag taking the liq out due to lack of funds as it signals users you are out of money which could lead to team token sales to fund the project coming from the communities pockets.

62484686
>>62484514
Magic hasn’t been in the tg for probably a year now? It’s said he hangs out in discord but the discord seemed dead last time I checked. He was also supposedly heavily involved with V2s development. I don’t know who is left anymore because I can’t even join the tg.

>>62484570
>don't listen to the haters stixil. If you keep trying you will eventually succeed and show everyone was wrong
Falko? Anyways, I ask in good faith. Besides the obvious trolling, what has been said so far that is wrong? Stixil clearly lacks any direction for where he wants to take this project. He has jumped from one idea to another any time something doesn’t work out. When V2 launched and got no traction, he immediately announced that they will take over stratus and for us to ‘stay tuned’. When that eventually doesn’t work out, he will have already came up with another new thing to keep people hanging on. It’s been like that for the last 3 years and it will continue being that way for as long as avi is around

62484716
>>62481088
They rebranded, increased token supply for themselves and airdropped everyone the new tokens.

62485941
>>62483861
>the fake pump did t even hold for 24 hours
I hope no one thought that mini pump was organic. It is just one of many that happened in the last couple of years and it’s probably a tg member trying to farm liquidity. It’s sad that this is what the volume is based on. Some bot / poorfag trying to farm $10-$50 from some other poorfags who got baited into buying a green chart lol

62486102
where is the fucking liquidity i cant sell what the fuck i need my money now. airfox? really? where is my cent per AVI that was promised to me

62486284
>>62486102
There is $11k liquidity. Thats more than enough

62486513
>$300k mcap
Yea I’m thinking it’s over

62486525
>>62486513
$80m whale is starting to sell. A former six figure bag is now worth $4k. Ouch

62487100
>>62484514
The Schizo also won and the red baron roped.
Flawless Victory

>>62481067
>>62481088
Anon, crypto is dead just as this board.
can't you fucking tell this already after two years?
The days of making 5 figs or even 3 figs on ETH shitcoins is over.
retail sadly migrated to solana and crime is legal now
those days of quick money on ETH and biz shitcoins are not coming back

62487573
>>62486525
a few bags were worth 7 figs at the height (yeah yeah, muh liquidity, I know) silly fuckers held all this time, that's gotta hurt, a few got out with 6 figs but fucked the chart

62487601
>>62486284
whoa, wtf? hadn't even spotted that, 9k now.....pffffffhahahahah

62487664
>>62487100
Solana is part of why crypto got fucked imo. Outside of the scammers and a few degens, I can’t imagine too many people making money there. But yea I don’t see any light at the end of the tunnel for crypto as a whole

item.title: Chainlink influencers panic as Link nowhere — 33 replies — 132.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlink influencers panic as Link nowhere

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-07-16 10:22 UTC

OP

to be found in much anticipated DTCC announcement. Apparently it's another conspiracy...

Early replies (first 3)

62484148
now ask yourself this, what would your reaction be if the shoe was on the other foot?

in other words, if the dtcc post had quoted chainlink accounts instead of xrp? kind of odd isn't it?

62484149
Peel thread

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62484160
as you can expect, top chainlink kol's woke up today in sheer terror seeing the announcement and now are working hard on the spin

apparently the dtcc was in on le conspiracy this time?

62484163
>>62484150
thats funny you got rattled so hard these past few years you have that one ready to go


bro you're not doing so hot are ya?


THE Cuckolds of crypto

62484176
>duuuuuuuude they just chose random tweets

so let me ask, why not a single tweet about chainlink?

oh let me guess, because:

>duuuuuuuuuuuuuuuude itsssss le SECRET

HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAH

62484189
token not needed

62484191
>>62484163
Try not to shit yourself today!

62484207
>>62484150
I'm happy for you or sorry that happened

62484229
>>62484191
you'll be ok buddy

just start with a few days a week at the gym

62484237
>>62484207
>I'm happy for you or sorry that happened


c'mon man you really going to recycle that line that's been said millions of times.

right up there with h heh my wifes boyfriend. go back bitch

62484287
>>62484148
but we know that chainlink is integrated and XRPL isnt. This is obviously free engagement from the retard army. Notice how none of the tweets actually mention XRP and their conspiracy theories? DTCC knows what theyre doing.

62484292
>>62484176
>so let me ask, why not a single tweet about chainlink?
The tweets arent about XRP, just from XRP accounts.

62484299
>>62484229
Do you need diapers when youre at the gym? I wouldnt want to use the equipment after a poopy pants touches it!

62484319
>>62484143
Do my eyes deceive me?
Yesterday the board was covered in unironic xrp gooning at the prospect that DTCC would have something to do with it

The verbose poster even showed himself again

62484370
>duuuuuuuuuude they choose at random and all randomly was xrp holders

bahahahahha

why is it so hard for the boyscouts to be honest? just admit that if the announcement had your loser kols included you would've been ecstatic


the harder a boyscout has to project his feigned arrogance, the more rage builds inside.

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62484374
>>62484143
KABOOOM

62484375
>>62484287
>This is obviously free engagement from the retard army

bro the post was made by the actual dtcc.


> we know that chainlink is integrated and XRPL isnt

no one cares about that buzzword shit boy. when it comes to cl, all of their so called integrations and partnerships and live products are marketing gimmicks

62484378
>>62484292
>The tweets arent about XRP, just from XRP accounts.


agreed. the question is what would the cult reaction be if they had instead come from chainlink kol accounts

fyi i dont care about your gay semantic tech arguments with xrp cult

62484400
>>62484375
>bro the post was made by the actual dtcc.
Yes now its being spread heavily by the XRP community, its actually great marketing and doesn't imply that the DTCC isnt even using XRPL (because they arent). They have posted on their own website what networks they support.

>no one cares about that buzzword shit boy
https://www.dtcc.com/news/2026/may/12/dtcc-collaborates-with-chainlink-to-advance-24-7-collateral-management

Can we see XRPLs integration news?

>instead come from chainlink kol accounts
It really doesn't matter what accounts it came from.

62484425
Its actually pretty funny they didnt use any tweets from community accounts that are confirmed to be integrated. Its next level trolling.

62484443
>>62484143
>>62484160
>>62484176

Remember when Adem/Thomas used to pretend he wasn't a xerpie?
So glad you went mask off at last, that's one layer peeled at least.

62484554
kek

62485182
https://www.dtcc.com/news/2026/july/15/dtcc-turns-tokenization-into-reality

lol
lmao

62485195
>>62485182
my village is hungry, when can chainlink feed us?

62485437
>>62484378
>the question is what would the cult reaction be if they had instead come from chainlink kol accounts
What is even the point of this question? Spit it out, vagueboy

62486773
>>62485182
But...... uhhhh............ price?? Oh.....and... chart???

62487540
>>62484150
it will never not be funny that every single fudkek out there has turned out, without exception, to be a lifeless husk with several addictions and arrested development. the cream of the crop, this fud entourage.

62487577
>>62487540
as opposed to linktards who are living a fullfilling life

62487617
>>62487577
actually, yes, we do
>posts screencap of a fudster larping as broken linkie
yawn. do you keep saving these? kek

62487623
>>62487617

62487634
>>62487623

There is your problem

62487662

item.title: memes aside, is LINK in an accumulation zone below $8? — 22 replies — 88.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
memes aside, is LINK in an accumulation zone below $8?

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-07-16 10:19 UTC

OP

memes aside, is LINK in an accumulation zone below $8?

Early replies (first 3)

62475926
>>62475875
Token doesn't have value in long term so no.

62475929
p+c

62476525
>>62475875
it's going to $4 by december and it won't clear $20 in the next cycle

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62476532
if you want to accumulate poop, sure

62476558
I'm buying at $3 later this year

62476560
>>62475875
Memes aside?
Probably, maybe.

Realistically yes, all crypto is in an accumulation zone right now

62476582
>>62475875
All of crypto is in accumulation zone right now. The only reason I’m not buying is my bags were loaded up years ago, and I’m buying other things

62476591
Every time LINK goes low I really want to accumulate but I never do, and then a year later I'm always really, really glad that I didn't. So I'll just stick to that strategy

62477482
>>62475875
No. Unironically maybe below $2 which is where revenues actually make sense vs. subsidized network bloat. Still would not buy; there is zero actual source of demand no matter how successful CLL ever become.

62477810
>>62475875
No, not until they settle the equity overhang.

62477824
>>62475875
$4.50 and $7 is the sweet spot

62479355
>>62475875
You can buy this at $3 by the end of the year

62480689
>>62475875
bery good token kind sir please buy on binance now

62480741
>>62475875
It accumulates losers

62480748
>>62480741
bitch bloody bastard bulgarian

62480766
I'll buy at $2 early next year

62480790
>>62480748
I'll kill you for this

62485179
yes accumulate as much as you can and trust Sergey, he would never lie or scam you ;)

62486870
Wow. The vitriol is palpable. Christ is king. Mufasa manifests municple marmalade.

62487584
>>62486870
mufasa 77777 my stinky teeth stay super stinky wagmi

62487649
>>62487584
You guys are really fucking bored aren't you

62487653
>>62487649
is your mouth still smelly? You're going to give girls the ICK

item.title: The gayest thing about this world is how poors are allowed to have things I can't buy. — 17 replies — 68.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The gayest thing about this world is how poors are allowed to have things I can't buy.

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-07-16 10:14 UTC

OP

The gayest thing about this world is how poors are allowed to have things I can't buy. If a poor has an IQ of 180, he can lord it over me and I can't do anything about it because I can't just buy an IQ of 180. Another example is that some poor probably nutted in prime Hailee Steinfeld and gets to keep those memories, but I can never buy those memories. Being well-off is insanely gay and unfair when it comes to stuff you can't buy. Fuck poors.

Early replies (first 3)

62485759
>>62485749
so you're ugly and stupid and can't talk to girls but at least you're "well-off"?

I bet you're not even well-off. Just ugly and stupid and can't talk to girls.

62485767
>>62485759
False. I'm smart and have fucked attractive women. I'm trying to make a general point here about money not going far enough.

62485772
>>62485767
you just sound stupid and ugly and unable to talk to girls
also poor

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62485880
>>62485749
Even better yet I can rent a nice car, fancy suit and pretend to be rich and get whatever benefit of the rich while getting off on tricking greedy degenerate losers into thinking they're associating with an important wealthy person

62485905
>>62485880
4chins should be proud of people like you Anon. Seriously, that's the spirit. Unironically.

62485954
>>62485749
We're 30 years away from the brain tissue industry anon. Millions of poors will let their brain get harvested a bit at a time so the privileged can keep building up an ever improving giga brain. Keep hustling and you'll eventually be able to hop in. Hell, being smarter makes you a better investor, so the more poor brain you implant, the faster you'll be able to grab more of these neurons. At some point there will be a caste of people so profoundly intelligent they will be able to figure out how to cure aging and create a post-scarcity utopia (for them only), from which sealed enviromentally-regulated domes they will be able to keep leeching smarts into their enormous bulbous heads eternally. That could be you, anon, just saying. I'm personally going to stop myself at 500 IQ or so because hyperintelligent people tend to become depressed fags and kill themselves, but I'm very optimistic with everything so I think I can safely take a few hundreds in boosted IQ.

62485986
>>62485954
It's not the IQ itself that causes depression

there are a handful of truths that destroy your will to live, and they become obvious to intelligent people at various IQ's
It's quite likely that IQ 130 is sufficient to kill you, it just might take a couple decades longer than someone with IQ 160
there is no real safe level above about 120. That's approximately where you need to be in order to see through the bullshit.

62485988
>>62485749
I wonder if the OP is a janny. I bet he is.
what a sad, pathetic existence.

62485994
>>62485988
obviously.

If he's IQdelet from /pmg/ as seems likely, he's been doing this for 5 years now without any pushback from 4chan.

62486106
>>62485986
Then I only need to keep cycling drugs every time I get sad until I get knocked down to just a standart deviation above, and then all they way to 500 again and keep looping.

62486140
>>62486106
your english comprehension is very poor

62486358
>>62485986
Secret is to also be jacked and attractive.

62487534
>>62486140
It will stop being so poor when I improve my brain at the expense of minimum wage workers and the homeless!

62487592
>>62485767
>>62485749
You sound brown

62487596
>>62485767
>money not going far enough
It never is.
Just look at Musk, do you think he gets everything he wants in life, while being the first public trillionaire?

62487615
>>62485749
Money isn't everything.

62487636
>>62485749
The gayest thing about this world is how men with small dicks are allowed to fuck my pass. If a small dick person has a 4" penis, he can lord it over me and I can't do anything about it because I'm gay and my name is OCPD. Another example is that some small dick person probably nutted in prime My Ass and gets to keep those memories, but I can never buy those memories. Being well-endowed with a large cock is insanely gay and unfair when it comes to stuff you can't fuck. Fuck people with small dicks.

>this thread was posted by a balding 40 year old discord tranny who previously nameniggered on /biz/ as OCPD. he posts the same shit posts and shit threads 12+ hours a day, every day. you can pick out his garbage posts by his autistic obsession with "the bottom 90%" and "brokies." he frequently changes IDs and replies to himself in his own threads by using the leto pedophile furfag residential VPN shitposting proxy website.

item.content:encoded:
Who's still buying BTC at this level?

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-07-16 09:27 UTC

OP

BTC has already come a long way. Are you still buying here, waiting for a pullback, or just holding what you already have?

>Curious what everyone's plan is.

Early replies (first 3)

62475243
waiting for $10k and I will buy 3 btc

62475257
>>62475235
YOUR MOTHER

62475258
>>62475235
I'm DCAing bitcoin at the second half of the midterm year.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62475971
>>62475243
Waiting for 10k? Are you serious?

62476143
>>62475235
I DCA yes

62476227
>>62476143
do it everyday

62476239
>>62475258
It's scary how Ben is always right.

62476359
>>62476239
Ben is always right . Every day . All tweets are right

62476425
>>62475235
>Who's still buying BTC at this level?
One of the biggest corporation and all the billionaires in the world?
Also
ME?
On Top of all, there's 240,000 fucking BTC being traded everyday.
There's less than 944,000 Bitcoin to be mined left.
Make your math.

62477616
>>62476425
You're broke

62477948
>>62477616
What makes you think that?

62477951
>>62477616
I know him in real life, he's loaded.

62479061
>>62477951
Ben??

62479203
>>62475971
When 10k or 5k

62479569
>>62475235
Time to buy bitcoin

62480040
>>62479203
delusional peg

62480706
>>62477951
You are him.

62480931
im buying its the cheapest it will ever get!

62481737
>>62480931
it will never go cheap

62481842
buy now

62483293
>>62481737
What's the best entry buying

62483295
Look for Robinhood chainnnnnnnnnn

62483495
>>62476425
You're broke.

62483652
>>62483495
broke and broken hearted

62483662
>>62475235
Tomorrow will be a nice day for buying, he will dump it just like the last time

62484050
>>62483662
Short-term moves don't bother me much anymore. I'll keep accumulating and natively staking my BTC.

62484717
>>62484050
Do you have your BTC on a TBV? or how are you staking it natively?

62486316
>>62483293
Wait for 60k

62486462
>>62475258
been DCA at $100/week for a while. Total spent is $17,400 and total value is $17,888.

62486528
>>62483293
DCAing into Bitcoin in the second half of a midterm year usually works out pretty well. Remember, just knowing we're in a bear market isn't enough, you have to take action. You aren't going to time the exact bottom, but slowly accumulating when prices are low is how you navigate bear markets.

62486684
>>62484717
TBVs are still in the public testnet phase on Aave V4. Hopefully we'll see a launch later this year.

62487150
>>62486684
It's a boring market

62487536
>>62487150
How do you know? It's still in testnet. They're literally testing the use cases. Plenty of potential if it works.

item.title: >meet pudgy mid-30s guy at crypto conference >tells me he is all-in on LINK >assume he's a /biz/raeli and call him a sti… — 9 replies — 27.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
>meet pudgy mid-30s guy at crypto conference >tells me he is all-in on LINK >assume he's a /biz/raeli and call him a sti…

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-07-16 10:07 UTC

OP

>meet pudgy mid-30s guy at crypto conference
>tells me he is all-in on LINK
>assume he's a /biz/raeli and call him a stinky linkie as a joke
>turns out he's not. he gets genuinely offended and contacts the event organizers to try to have me kicked out of there for harrassment

Early replies (first 3)

62479851
>>62479839
>at crypto conference
yeah your fault

62480066
>>62479839
>>tells me he is all-in on LINK
he got brutally fucked by the market over the last 6 years

62482550
>>62480066
>by the market
lol
lmao

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62482964
>>62479839
Lmfao Reminds me of a recent post I saw of a linkie posting in an XRP thread where he accidentally copypasted a legitimately copypasta worthy post which to caricaturize XRP holders, BUT THE POST HE COPIED WAS AN EARNESTLY INTENDED REAL LINK POSTER LOLCOW. Lol:
https://warosu.org/biz/thread/62481384#p62482676
>BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM SHAKALA. I am new to 4chan but have been loving this meme lately! I read on reddit yesterday that this would happen and I boughted (but unfortunately the price basically immediately soon after). I was still so pumped I went to Costco and got a slice of pizza and sat facing those walking out looking at everyone walking by knowing and rye-ly smiling knowing none of them understood what is happening. I felt like Brownstone Capital with Ben Rickerts in the Big Short. I had to stop myself from celebrating because I remembered they were missing out and I wanted to feel the same type of compassion they were reminded to feel by Ben in that moment so I finished my pizza solemnly leaving a bite out of clenched stomach respect for them not being able to finish my meal, grabbed my bag, put my hood up, and walked out nodding to the door inspector knowing looking at them knowingly and humanly as I walked out with them smiling not knowing beneath my shades how I was really seeing them. It was an amazing experience and it felt like everyone was watching but I know they weren't because the thing that made it special was that they don't understand or see. I will come back and tip the kitchen staff handsomely (especially the girl who I like but have not had the courage to ask out....yet) when we are making bookoo bucks and I can afford to own the whole store and promote her. and then smirk as I tell her I will sell the store, non-chalantly, so that I can ask her so she would feel comfortable if I ask her out on a date after I made her general manager of the store. I can't wait!
This is what literally all link investors are actually like.

62482966
>>62479839
>>62482964
cont.
It is a really weird cult. The whole thread the post originally was posted in is actually hilarious and worth reading.

62483048
fuck stinks hate stinks despise stinks detest stinks loathe stinks fuck stinks hate stinks despise stinks detest stinks

62485675
>>62479839
imagine what a horrible life that baggie has

62487614
>>62479839
80% chance he was an indian

62487626
Smells like sharted underwear in here, IBSanon has been busy

item.title: Should I just dump my life savings of 250k into VOO? — 15 replies — 25.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Should I just dump my life savings of 250k into VOO?

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-07-16 09:52 UTC

OP

Should I just dump my life savings of 250k into VOO? I'm 39 and wondering if I'm running out of time

Early replies (first 3)

62483575
Probably, as your window for having time to recover from higher risk gambles is closing rapidly, but you needed more than that in there by the time you are 39 for it to mean anything by retirement....

62483594
Stay strong and wait for the crash

62483632
>>62483560
sure after you hit a 10x
else you are too late yes indeed you are not a zoomie you are unc

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62486749
>>62483560
You should have dumped 250k into VOO at 29.

62486755
im almost 39 and still gambling on shitcoins

who cares we all die in the end anyway, im here to make money now and enjoy, not die with a big number in my bank account

62486762
>>62483560
I hope you have a paid off house

62486778
>>62483560
the market is about to dump 50-60% so if you want to cut that in half sure go ahead.

62486872
>>62483632
Hello saar do the english

62486886
>>62486749
>should have bought amazon in 1988

62486894
>>62486886

62487002
VOO will dump soon. Could take decades to round trip.

62487006
how about

50% qqq
50% schd

62487027
don't dump it all. Buy some now like 10k and just keep adding more on a schedule. Maybe buy more when a big drop happens.

62487458
>>62483560
>Should I just dump my life savings of 250k into VOO
No.

62487533
>>62483560
Anon you need to take bigger risks
Try VIGAX or VGT which are still diversified enough to avoid a single bankruptcy destroying them but earn much higher than VOO due to heavy tech focus

62484437
>>62483350

what will the floor be next week? 110?

62484447

62484508
>>62483350

62485058
>>62484508

62485272

62485350

62485635
>>62484437

62486516

he doesn't know
kek good goy

62486545
>>62483350
No it won't, kessler syndrome hasn't been solved. And until you can guarantee that Earth's orbit won't turn into a wall of scrap moving at several times the speed sound, trapping us on the planet. The whole space thing is nothing but speculation. Anyone with enough rocket tech to get to low earth orbit can make space exploration impossible. Wake me when the orbital defenses get built.

62486606
>>62483366
Good goyim

62486621
>>62483350

62486626

62486686

62486692

62486741

>>62484127

62487473
>>62483350

62487479

Haven't changed my mind yet but that number might not be 135.

item.title: NWO 2030: you will own and be happy — 9 replies — 16.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
NWO 2030: you will own and be happy

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-07-16 09:55 UTC

OP

Is this bullshit intentional?
The job market and economy is so ass and everyone saying that this is the new normal and it will never get better. Fuck kikes man. Is this worse than 2008 job market? I was 6 so idk

Early replies (first 3)

62485237
>>62485209
You were 6 in 2008? You know you have to be 18 to post here right?!

62485262
>>62485237
Check the calander unc.

62485943
>>62485209
>Agenda 2030
Normies forgot about that and thought we were in the clear. Sucks to suck if ya got a shit memory, oh well

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62485965
>>62485943
We are in the clear. President Trump won and the NWO is basically defeated

62485981
>>62485965
This. As long as we keep the Dems out of office the future for America is actually very bright

62487465
You would own wife maid and children and be genuinely happy the connection.

62487568
>>62485209
first women ruined politics
now they ruined the job market

62487572
>>62487457
Disrespect to the guy or bot who keeps posting this shit in every thread

62487588
I've made threads about this before, but hardly anyone listens, I guess because I can't safely verify what I've heard firsthand. But what I'm about to say is actual fact. Basically, a network of very high-level elites sees itself in a long-term arms race with the "non-productive" class. Depending on how tech, culture, and politics play out, the non-productive class could dispossess and perhaps even kill (a la Russia and China in the 20th century) the "productive" class. The elites I mentioned are trying to figure out how to preemptively disarm the non-productive class as "ethically" as possible, so that it can be contained long-term and put on some kind of UBI. The end goal is for the non-productive to live decent lives but be permanently unable to harm or dispossess the productive.
